document.title: IEC 62133 CB Test Report and CB Test Certificate for Rechargeable Li-ion Battery Pack ELT-3S1PA / 3ICP5/43/97
document.documentType: CB Test Report, CB Test Certificate, and amendment attachment
document.revisionOrVersion: Amendment 1 / BA-4790123409-A-1; Original CBTR HCT-BA-1801-CB002; CBTC DK-70440-UL
standards: UN Manual of Tests and Criteria Section 38.3

sourceSummary.oneLine: Chunk documents IEC 62133 CB safety certification evidence for rechargeable Li-ion battery pack ELT-3S1PA / 3ICP5/43/97, including original CBTR results, CB Test Certificate DK-70440-UL, and Amendment 1 component/applicant updates.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Use as regulatory evidence for battery safety conformity, CB Scheme certification traceability, critical component control, and lithium battery transport documentation references.
keyPoints: The original IEC 62133 CB test report HCT-BA-1801-CB002 was issued on 2018-02-01 and supports CB Test Certificate DK-70440-UL issued on 2018-02-05.
The tested rechargeable Li-ion battery pack model is ELT-3S1PA / 3ICP5/43/97, rated 11.55 Vdc, 3200 mAh, 36.96 Wh.

Original testing reported compliance with IEC 62133:2012 and EN 62133:2013, with lithium battery tests including moulded case stress, external short circuit, free fall, overcharge, and transport documentation review.

Amendment 1 revised applicant and factory name/address, added Control IC bq40z50-R1 and FET AON6590A, deleted the explicit IEC/EN fulfilment sentence, and stated no tests were deemed necessary by engineering consideration.

entities.name: Elentec Co., Ltd / ELENTEC CO LTD
entities.evidence: Applicant/manufacturer/factory is listed as Elentec Co., Ltd in the original report and revised to ELENTEC CO LTD at 64-3, Dongtangiheung-ro, Hwaseong-si, Gyeonggi-do, 18487, Republic of Korea in Amendment 1.

entities.name: Underwriters Laboratories Taiwan Co., Ltd.
entities.evidence: Amendment report page 2 identifies the CB Testing Laboratory as Underwriters Laboratories Taiwan Co., Ltd., 260 Da-Yeh Road 112 Peitou Taipei City, Chinese Taipei.

entities.name: HCT Co., Ltd.
entities.evidence: Original report summary lists testing location as HCT Co., Ltd., 74, Seoicheon-ro 578 beon-gil, Majang-myeon, Icheon-si, Gyeonggi-do, Korea.

entities.name: UL
entities.evidence: CB Test Certificate states it is issued by the National Certification Body UL with UL US, UL Demko, UL JP, and UL CA addresses.

entities.name: SAMSUNG SDI CO., LTD.
entities.evidence: Critical components table lists the lithium-ion rechargeable battery cell manufacturer as SAMSUNG SDI CO., LTD., model PGF494397H++.

entities.name: TEXAS INSTRUMENTS
entities.evidence: Critical components table lists Control IC U1 from TEXAS INSTRUMENTS, type bq40z50-R2 / R1.

entities.name: ALPHA & OMEGA SEMICONDUCTOR
entities.evidence: Critical components table lists FET Q1,Q2 from ALPHA & OMEGA SEMICONDUCTOR, type AON6590(A).

concepts.name: CB Test Report validity
concepts.evidence: The report states it is not valid as a CB Test Report unless signed by an approved CB Testing Laboratory and appended to a CB Test Certificate issued by an NCB in accordance with IECEE 02.

concepts.name: IEC 62133 lithium battery safety conformity
concepts.evidence: The original report lists IEC 62133 lithium system clauses with pass verdicts and reports no fire or explosion for external short circuit, free fall, and overcharge battery tests.

concepts.name: Amendment without retesting
concepts.evidence: Amendment 1 states component and applicant/factory updates and concludes that no tests were deemed necessary by engineering consideration.

concepts.name: Critical component control
concepts.pageTarget: Page 7 of 7 Report No. BA-4790123409-A-1; Page 16 of 26 Report No. HCT-BA-1801-CB002
concepts.evidence: Critical components tables identify battery cell, PCB, control IC, protection IC, FET, fuse, PTC, thermistors, and enclosure with manufacturers, ratings, standards, and conformity marks.

concepts.name: UN 38.3 transport documentation
concepts.evidence: Clause 8.3.8 states manufacturer documentation was provided to show compliance with UN Recommendations on Transport of Dangerous Goods, with enclosure pages labeled UN manual of tests and criteria, Section 38.3.

evidence.claim: The test item is a rechargeable Li-ion battery pack model ELT-3S1PA / 3ICP5/43/97 rated 11.55 Vdc, 3200 mAh, 36.96 Wh.
evidence.evidenceText: Test item description: Rechargeable Li-ion Battery Pack; Model/Type reference: ELT-3S1PA / 3ICP5/43/97; Ratings: 11.55 Vdc, 3200 mAh, 36.96 Wh.
evidence.pageTarget: Page 2 of 7 Report No. BA-4790123409-A-1; original report title page

evidence.claim: Original CB Test Report HCT-BA-1801-CB002 was issued under IEC 62133:2012 using the CB Scheme.
evidence.evidenceText: Report Number: HCT-BA-1801-CB002; Date of issue: 2018-02-01; Standard: IEC 62133: 2012 (Second Edition); Test procedure: CB Scheme.

evidence.claim: CB Test Certificate DK-70440-UL was issued on 2018-02-05 for the rechargeable Li-ion battery pack and references test report HCT-BA-1801-CB002 issued on 2018-02-01.
evidence.evidenceText: Ref. Certif. No. DK-70440-UL; Date: 2018-02-05; Test Report Ref. No. HCT-BA-1801-CB002 issued on 2018-02-01.

evidence.claim: The original report states the product fulfilled IEC 62133:2012 and EN 62133:2013 requirements.
evidence.evidenceText: Summary of testing states: The product fulfils the requirements of IEC 62133:2012 and EN 62133:2013.

evidence.claim: Amendment 1 changed applicant and factory name/address to ELENTEC CO LTD in Hwaseong-si, Republic of Korea.
evidence.evidenceText: Amendment 1 states revision from Elentec Co., Ltd / Dongtangihung-ro Dongtan-myeon address to ELENTEC CO LTD / 64-3, Dongtangiheung-ro, Hwaseong-si, Gyeonggi-do, 18487, Republic of Korea.

evidence.claim: Amendment 1 added Control IC bq40z50-R1 and FET AON6590A with no specification change from prior listed parts.
evidence.evidenceText: Amendment 1 says: Add Control IC, type bq40z50-R1 manufactured by TI, no specification change from bq40z50-R2; Add FET, type AON6590A manufactured by AOS, no specification change from AON6590.

evidence.claim: Amendment 1 required no additional tests by engineering consideration.
evidence.evidenceText: Amendment 1 final bullet states: No tests were deemed necessary by engineering consideration.

evidence.claim: Battery charging parameters include CC/CV charging, 8.25 Vdc final voltage, 4.45 Vdc upper limit charging voltage per cell, 2800 mA maximum charging current, and 10 to 45 °C charging temperature limits.
evidence.evidenceText: Test item particulars list CC/CV, specified final voltage 8.25 Vdc, upper limit charging voltage 4.45 Vdc per cell, maximum charging current 2800 mA, charging temperature upper 45 °C and lower 10 °C.
evidence.pageTarget: Page 5 of 7 Report No. BA-4790123409-A-1; Page 5 of 26 Report No. HCT-BA-1801-CB002

evidence.claim: Original testing included IEC 62133 clauses 8.2.2, 8.3.2, 8.3.3, and 8.3.6.
evidence.evidenceText: Summary of testing lists tests performed: 8.2.2 Moulded case stress, 8.3.2 External short circuit, 8.3.3 Free fall, 8.3.6 Overcharge.

evidence.claim: External short circuit battery testing passed with no fire or explosion at upper and lower charging temperature sample conditions.
evidence.evidenceText: Clause 8.3.2 has verdict P and the table lists ELT-3S1PA samples charged at 45 °C and 10 °C with results: No fire or explosion.
evidence.pageTarget: Page 12 and Page 22 of 26 Report No. HCT-BA-1801-CB002

evidence.claim: Over-charging battery testing passed with no fire or explosion.
evidence.evidenceText: Clause 8.3.6 Over-charging of battery has verdict P; table lists constant charging current 6.40 A, supply voltage 15.0 Vdc, and ELT-3S1PA samples OC-1 to OC-5 with results: No fire or explosion.
evidence.pageTarget: Page 12 and Page 24 of 26 Report No. HCT-BA-1801-CB002

evidence.claim: The battery was marked with designation 3ICP5/43/97 as the IEC 62133 marking.
evidence.evidenceText: General product information states the designation 3ICP5/43/97 is the marking of IEC 62133 requirement for the battery; clause 10.2 battery marking lists 3ICP5/43/97 with pass verdict.
evidence.pageTarget: Page 6 of 26 and Page 13 of 26 Report No. HCT-BA-1801-CB002

evidence.claim: The lithium-ion rechargeable battery cell PGF494397H++ was supported by IEC 62133 evidence and DEKRA certificate reference NL-44284.
evidence.evidenceText: Critical components table lists Samsung SDI PGF494397H++, 3.85 Vdc, 3600 mAh, standard IEC 62133:2012, DEKRA Ref. Certif. No. NL-44284.

evidence.claim: The original report enclosure package included circuit diagrams, user recommendations, package instructions, battery specifications, quality management certificate, photographs, and UN 38.3 transport test report material.
evidence.evidenceText: Enclosures list diagrams, recommendations to equipment manufacturers and end-users, package instructions, specifications of battery, quality management system certificate, photographs, and test report for transport UN manual of tests and criteria Section 38.3.
evidence.pageTarget: Page 1 of 20 Enclosures, Report No. HCT-BA-1801-CB002

reviewFlags.reason: The source chunk is the final chunk and contains appended battery CBTR/CBTC material plus Attachment 8 diagrams; it may be only one attachment within a larger medical device safety/EMC source file.

Amendment 1 explicitly deletes the original sentence that the product fulfils IEC 62133:2012 and EN 62133:2013, so downstream summaries should distinguish original CBTR wording from the amended report wording.
Several CB Test Certificate lines appear OCR-encoded, but certificate number DK-70440-UL, product, model, rating, report reference, and issue date remain legible.
